steps.title: Identify the key distinction
steps.content: The passage emphasizes the difference between sleep and boredom—the former is an effect Apichatpong intentionally produces, while the latter causes anxiety. This distinction matters because it shows that Apichatpong calling his films “sleep inducing” is not self-deprecation.

steps.title: Apply the distinction to identify the main idea
steps.content: Choice D states the main point: interpreting Apichatpong's description as self-criticism would be a misunderstanding, because the effects are intentional. The other three misread the passage by predicting audience outcomes or distinguishing between filmmakers, neither of which is the passage's focus. That matches choice D — D is correct.
